reasoning: The baseline metric (1 + 30-day average of fatalities over the prior 360 days) is 1.000, meaning there were 0 ACLED-recorded fatalities in Curacao over the preceding year. To resolve YES, the 30-day period before each resolution date must see more than 10 times this baseline (i.e. >10 fatalities, meaning 11 or more). Curacao is a highly stable country with single-digit total homicides in most years, and ACLED only tracks political/gang violence, making it even rarer. A search of recent news up to Feb 1, 2026, confirms no mass casualty events or large-scale violence. The U.S. Consulate also recently confirmed there is no military spillover in the ABC islands from regional tensions. A sudden explosion of 11+ political/gang fatalities in a 1-week or 1-month window is a black swan event. Given the extreme lack of precedent and no recent leading indicators, the probability is essentially zero, but I will apply the minimum allowed probability of 0.05 for the near term, scaling very slightly to 0.06 for the 1-month horizon to reflect standard time-based uncertainty.
